description:
## Parent

bsm-7en — Render selected issue details in the issue explorer

## Background and intent

Earlier beads in this epic should include fast unit, adapter/RPC/Effect, and component/integration tests where possible. This final bead adds the real desktop proof: launch the built Tauri app against a disposable Beadwork workspace, click an Issue in the Issue List, and verify that selected Issue Detail content appears through the full Rust adapter → TauRPC → Effect → React path.

The existing Issue List WebDriver suite already launches the real debug desktop binary rather than a renderer-only mock. This bead should extend that suite instead of creating a parallel E2E surface. The test should remain focused on one representative user path and avoid brittle visual assertions.

This bead also cleans up documentation so future agents see the post-epic vocabulary and test behavior: `Issue` / `list_issues`, selectable Issue List, and Issue Detail rendering.

## What to build

Extend the existing WebDriver Issue List fixture and success scenario so it creates a disposable Beadwork workspace with an Issue that has enough data to prove detail rendering after selection. The scenario should launch the real desktop app, wait for the Issue List, click an Issue row, and assert representative Issue Detail content is visible.

The E2E assertion set should prove the completed path without overfitting implementation details. Representative content should include enough of the completed feature to catch integration regressions, such as selected title/ID, Markdown description output, dependency context or metadata, and comment content.

Update WebDriver/E2E documentation and stale summary-oriented references so future agents understand the current suite and naming.

## Reasoning and constraints

- Keep E2E as its own final slice, per planning decision, because earlier slices can be verified faster with unit/component/integration tests.
- Extend the existing Issue List suite rather than creating a new suite unless the existing structure makes that impossible.
- The suite should launch the real built desktop binary using the existing WebDriver/debug build setup.
- Avoid asserting CSS implementation details or fragile layout internals. Assert user-visible outcomes.
- Existing empty-list E2E behavior must continue to pass.
- Do not add new product behavior in this bead; this is proof and documentation for the completed Issue Detail path.

## Testing guidance

Run the relevant WebDriver Issue List commands documented in the repo after updating the fixture/spec. If the upstream WebDriver caveats from the existing docs still apply, preserve or update those notes rather than rediscovering them later.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The existing Issue List WebDriver fixture includes an Issue description and comments suitable for detail-rendering assertions.
- [ ] The WebDriver success scenario launches the real desktop app against a disposable Beadwork workspace.
- [ ] The success scenario clicks an Issue row and verifies representative Issue Detail content renders through the full Rust adapter → TauRPC → Effect → React path.
- [ ] The E2E test stays focused on user-visible outcomes and avoids over-asserting visual implementation details.
- [ ] Existing empty-list E2E behavior continues to pass.
- [ ] WebDriver/E2E documentation is updated for the selected Issue Detail path, including how to run the suite and any known caveats that still apply.
- [ ] Stale `IssueSummary` / `list_issue_summaries` references in relevant docs are updated to `Issue` / `list_issues` language.
- [ ] No new application behavior is introduced only to satisfy the E2E test.

## Out of scope

- No new Issue Detail features beyond proving those already built by previous beads.
- No renderer-only E2E replacement for the real desktop path.
- No broad visual regression suite.
- No new workspace-switching, refresh, route, or mutation behavior.

## Blocked by

bsm-7en.2
bsm-7en.3
bsm-7en.4
bsm-7en.5

comments.text:
# Implementation Plan

## Goal

Extend the existing real-desktop Issue List WebDriver suite so it selects a Beadwork Issue and proves representative Issue Detail content renders through the current `list_issues` path, while updating E2E documentation and stale summary wording.

## Tasks

1. **Enrich the disposable Issue List fixture with detail-ready data**
   - File: `e2e/issue-list/fixtures/workspace.ts`
   - Changes:
     - Replace `FIXTURE_ISSUE_TITLE = "Render real issue summaries end to end"` with detail-era wording, e.g. `"Render selected issue details end to end"`.
     - Extract the blocker title into an exported constant, e.g. `FIXTURE_BLOCKER_TITLE`, so the spec can locate the blocker row and read its dynamic ID.
     - Add exported constants for stable assertion text, for example:
       - `FIXTURE_ISSUE_DESCRIPTION` containing Markdown with a heading, plain paragraph text, bullets, and inline code.
       - `FIXTURE_COMMENT_AUTHOR` / `FIXTURE_COMMENT_TEXT`.
       - Optionally a second comment author/text pair to satisfy the “comments” fixture requirement without asserting every comment in the E2E.
     - Add `"--description", FIXTURE_ISSUE_DESCRIPTION` to the `bw create` call for the main fixture Issue.
     - After the dependency is created, add `runBw(["comment", issueId, FIXTURE_COMMENT_TEXT, "--author", FIXTURE_COMMENT_AUTHOR], workspacePath)` and, if using a second comment, a second `bw comment` call.
     - Update the fixture comment above `createIssueListWorkspace()` so it says the workspace includes labels, a blocking dependency, Markdown description, and comments.
   - Acceptance:
     - The fixture still creates one main Issue plus its blocker in a disposable Beadwork workspace.
     - The main Issue has a Markdown description and at least one authored comment available via `bw list --all --json` / `list_issues`.
     - No workspace setup is moved out of `createIssueListWorkspace()` / `run-scenario.ts`.

2. **Add a focused click-to-detail WebDriver assertion to the existing success spec**
   - File: `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.success.spec.ts`
   - Changes:
     - Update the file header comment and `describe` label from “Issue List slice” language to “Issue explorer / selected Issue Detail” language; remove the stale `bsm-mq4.5` reference or replace it with current behavior-oriented wording.
     - Import the new fixture constants from `./fixtures/workspace.ts`.
     - Keep the existing native `TauRPC__list_issues` sanity test and row-rendering test.
     - Add a new `it("selects the fixture issue and renders representative detail content", ...)` after the row-rendering test.
     - In the new test:
       - Wait for the main issue row with `article[aria-label*="${FIXTURE_ISSUE_TITLE}"]`.
       - Wait for the blocker row with `article[aria-label*="${FIXTURE_BLOCKER_TITLE}"]`.
       - Read the main Issue ID from the main row’s `button[data-issue-id]` attribute.
       - Read the blocker ID from the blocker row’s `button[data-issue-id]` attribute.
       - Click the main issue row button.
       - Wait for `main[aria-label="Issue detail"]` to be displayed and for its text to include the selected title and ID; this avoids relying on the empty/detail `main` elements sharing the same aria-label.
       - Assert the detail text includes representative user-visible content only:
         - selected title and selected Issue ID;
         - `Description` section heading plus rendered Markdown text from the fixture description, such as the Markdown heading text, one bullet text, and `list_issues` inline-code text;
         - `Comments` section heading plus fixture comment author and comment body text;
         - `Dependencies`, `Blocked by`, and the dynamic blocker ID.
       - Do not assert exact comment timestamps, CSS classes, layout, or screenshots.
     - Leave the sidebar workspace-path test functionally unchanged, even though it uses an existing Tailwind selector, to avoid widening scope.
   - Acceptance:
     - The success scenario still proves the native RPC path.
     - The success scenario now clicks a real issue row and verifies representative Issue Detail content through the running desktop app.
     - New assertions use user-visible text, aria labels, semantic content, or existing `data-issue-id` anchors, not visual implementation details.

3. **Keep the empty-list scenario unchanged and compatible**
   - File: `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.empty.spec.ts`
   - Changes: none expected.
   - Acceptance:
     - The empty scenario still creates a true empty Beadwork workspace via the existing runner and still asserts the empty Issue List state.
     - No fixture changes for the success scenario leak into `createEmptyWorkspace()`.

4. **Update WebDriver/E2E documentation for the selected Issue Detail path**
   - File: `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md`
   - Changes:
     - Change the title from `WebDriver end-to-end: Issue List` to cover the Issue explorer or Issue List + selected Issue Detail path.
     - Re-scope the introduction from the old Issue List-only slice (`bsm-mq4.5`) to the current suite behavior: real desktop launch, disposable Beadwork workspace, `Issue` data from Rust adapter → TauRPC → Effect → React, issue row selection, and Issue Detail rendering.
     - In “How it works”, update the fixture bullet to mention `bw create --description` and `bw comment` in addition to labels and dependencies.
     - In the specs bullet, document that `issue-list.success.spec.ts` now invokes `TauRPC__list_issues`, waits for the Issue row, asserts row metadata, clicks the row, and asserts representative detail content: title/ID, Markdown description output, dependency context, and comments.
     - Preserve existing operational caveats and setup details: embedded WebDriver provider, port `46245`, `src-tauri/capabilities/webdriver-e2e.json`, `src-tauri/tauri.e2e.conf.json`, `VITE_BEADSMITH_E2E_WDIO=1`, and the pinned `@wdio/native-utils` override.
     - Rewrite “Deliberately out of scope” so Issue Detail itself is no longer listed as out of scope; keep broad visual regression, mutations, workspace switching, search, and filter coverage out of scope.
   - Acceptance:
     - A future agent can run and understand the selected Issue Detail E2E path from this doc.
     - The doc no longer says Issue Detail is out of scope for this suite.

5. **Verify and clean up stale summary-oriented vocabulary in relevant files**
   - Files: `e2e/issue-list/fixtures/workspace.ts`, `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.success.spec.ts`, `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md`; search across `docs/`, `e2e/`, `src/`, and `src-tauri/`.
   - Changes:
     - Run a search equivalent to `rg "IssueSummary|list_issue_summaries|issue summaries" docs e2e src src-tauri`.
     - Update user-facing stale references to `Issue` / `list_issues` / selected Issue Detail language.
     - Do not remove the concatenated anti-regression assertions in `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs` if encountered; they intentionally assert that generated bindings do not contain the old names and are not user-facing stale docs.
     - Treat research-stage PRD references to future Issue Detail as a separate documentation decision; only update them if they directly conflict with current E2E documentation expectations.
   - Acceptance:
     - No stale user-facing `IssueSummary`, `list_issue_summaries`, or “issue summaries” wording remains in the active E2E suite/docs.

6. **Run focused validation for the E2E suite and formatting**
   - Files: no source changes beyond the files above.
   - Changes: none; validation only.
   - Acceptance:
     - Run `pnpm dlx ultracite check` or the repo’s preferred equivalent to catch TypeScript/format issues in the changed files.
     - Run `pnpm e2e:build` after any Rust/frontend changes affecting the debug binary, or before E2E if no valid debug binary exists.
     - Run `pnpm e2e:issue-list:success` to prove the new selected Issue Detail user path.
     - Run `pnpm e2e:issue-list:empty` to prove the empty-list scenario still passes.
     - Optionally run `pnpm e2e:issue-list` or `pnpm test:e2e:issue-list` as a combined final check if time and local environment allow.
     - Re-run the stale vocabulary search from Task 5 and include the result in the implementation handoff.
     - Check `git diff --check` before final handoff.

## Files to Modify

- `e2e/issue-list/fixtures/workspace.ts` - refresh stale fixture title wording, add exported assertion constants, create the main fixture Issue with Markdown description, and add authored comments via `bw comment`.
- `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.success.spec.ts` - import new fixture constants and add a new WebDriver test that clicks the fixture Issue row and asserts representative Issue Detail content.
- `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md` - update suite scope, fixture/spec behavior, run guidance context, and out-of-scope language for selected Issue Detail coverage.

## New Files

- None.

## Dependencies

- Task 2 depends on Task 1 because the spec needs stable fixture constants and actual description/comment data to assert.
- Task 4 should be done after Tasks 1 and 2 so the documentation matches the implemented fixture/spec behavior.
- Task 5 should run after Tasks 1-4 so it can catch newly introduced stale vocabulary as well as existing text.
- Task 6 depends on Tasks 1-5 being complete.

## Risks

- `bw comment` timestamps are generated at fixture creation time; avoid asserting exact timestamps in WebDriver tests.
- Beadwork issue IDs are dynamic; read selected Issue and blocker IDs from `button[data-issue-id]` rather than hard-coding IDs.
- `main[aria-label="Issue detail"]` is used by both the empty and populated detail states; wait for selected title/ID inside the detail text after clicking to avoid racing the transition.
- The local `bw` binary on `PATH` must support `create --description` and `comment --author`; fixture creation will fail early if the local version is older or missing.
- Embedded WebDriver can fail if port `46245` is held by a stale debug app; preserve the existing port preflight behavior and document the caveat.
- Do not add new RPCs, workspace switching, route state, app behavior, screenshots, or CSS/layout assertions; this bead is proof for already-built Issue Detail rendering.
- It is underspecified whether metadata such as assignee or parent must be included. The concrete plan uses dependency context plus description/comments/title/ID, matching the acceptance criterion wording “dependency context or metadata”; add assignee/parent only if a reviewer explicitly asks for metadata coverage.

title: Extend desktop E2E coverage for Issue Detail
